originalTitle: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business ‘fair and square’
summary: Google officially filed its appeal of the federal ruling deeming it an illegal search monopolist, arguing the decision "crashed" through legal guardrails. "Google just prevailed in the marketplace fair and square," it writes in its legal filing. Google had already said it would appeal the ruling, which includes both the August 2024 decision about its […]
content: Google officially filed its appeal of the federal ruling deeming it an illegal search monopolist, arguing the decision “crashed” through legal guardrails. “Google just prevailed in the marketplace fair and square,” it writes in its legal filing. Google had already said it would appeal the ruling, which includes both the August 2024 decision about its illegal monopolization and the September 2025 remedies decision that ordered it to share some search data with competitors. The appeal brief filed Friday gives more insight into how the company plans to fight Judge Amit Mehta’s ruling. “We are asking the court to overturn this flawed decision – partners and users have many options and choose Google because it provides the best, most helpful results,” Google VP of regulatory affairs Lee-Anne Mulholland said in a statement. Google argues that Mehta erred in finding that its search distribution agreements with browser and device makers were anticompetitive. Instead, it argues, other market players simply preferred its services over rivals’. Mehta also “egregiously exceeded” his judicial discretion in the remedies he ordered, according to Google, which included the “extraordinary step of ordering Google to boost its competitors through data-transfer and syndication.” The company also takes issue with how the remedies require data sharing with generative AI players that it says “could not have been affected by Google’s conduct because they did not even exist during the relevant period, and that are already succeeding as wildly as any technology in human history without any need to free-ride on Google’s success.” The US and a coalition of states that sued alongside it are also appealing the same decision, arguing that Mehta should have gone further in his remedies decision. Mehta declined to grant the government its biggest asks, including a sale of Google’s Chrome browser, which it argues is a key distribution platform for search offerings. The government argued that a broad sweep of changes was necessary to resolve Google’s harms to competition. About five years since the initial case was filed, it’s now up to a federal appeals court in DC to determine what should happen next. From there, the case could eventually go all the way up to the Supreme Court.
chineseTitle: 谷歌对搜索垄断裁决提出上诉，称其“公平公正”地赢得了业务
chineseSummary: 谷歌正式对联邦裁决提出上诉，认为自己是非法搜索垄断者，并称该裁决“冲破”了法律护栏。 “谷歌在市场上公平公正地取得了胜利，”它在法律文件中写道。谷歌已经表示将对裁决提出上诉，其中包括 2024 年 8 月关于其 [...]
chineseContent: 谷歌正式对联邦裁决提出上诉，认为自己是非法搜索垄断者，并称该裁决“冲破”了法律护栏。 “谷歌在市场上公平公正地取得了胜利，”它在法律文件中写道。谷歌已经表示将对这项裁决提出上诉，其中包括 2024 年 8 月关于其非法垄断的裁决，以及 2025 年 9 月命令其与竞争对手共享一些搜索数据的补救措施裁决。周五提交的上诉简报让人们更深入地了解该公司计划如何对抗阿米特·梅塔法官的裁决。谷歌监管事务副总裁 Lee-Anne Mulholland 在一份声明中表示：“我们要求法院推翻这一有缺陷的决定——合作伙伴和用户有很多选择，选择谷歌是因为它提供了最好、最有帮助的结果。”谷歌辩称，梅塔错误地发现其与浏览器和设备制造商的搜索分销协议具有反竞争性。相反，它认为，其他市场参与者只是更喜欢它的服务而不是竞争对手的服务。据谷歌称，梅塔在他所下令的补救措施中也“严重超出了”他的司法自由裁量权，其中包括“命令谷歌通过数据传输和联合组织来增强竞争对手的非同寻常的步骤”。该公司还对补救措施如何要求与生成人工智能参与者共享数据表示质疑，称“不可能受到谷歌行为的影响，因为它们在相关时期甚至不存在，而且已经像人类历史上任何技术一样取得了巨大的成功，不需要搭谷歌的成功。”美国和与其一起提起诉讼的国家联盟也对同一决定提出上诉，认为梅塔应该在其补救措施决定中走得更远。梅塔
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
